edges.description: Failure of CuA assembly yields a catalytically inactive enzyme.
edges.source: SCO2 Loss and Defective Copper Delivery to COX
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in cardiomyocytes drives hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy deficit in the CNS produces the encephalopathy component of SCO2 cardioencephalomyopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Impaired oxidative phosphorylation shifts pyruvate metabolism toward lactate accumulation, producing lactic acidosis.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Encephalopathy as part of the cardioencephalomyopathy phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: we have identified mutations in the human homologue, SCO2, in three unrelated infants with a newly recognized fatal cardioencephalomyopathy and COX deficiency.

nodes.description: Cardiac hypertrophy, the hallmark feature of SCO2-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: we have identified mutations in the human homologue, SCO2, in three unrelated infants with a newly recognized fatal cardioencephalomyopathy and COX deficiency.

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis, with greatest impact on cardiac and skeletal muscle.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

nodes.description: Elevated lactate from impaired oxidative metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Cytochrome c oxidase deficiency: patients and animal models.
evidence.snippet: Human diseases associated with COX deficiency including encephalomyopathies, Leigh syndrome, hypertrophic cardiomyopathies, and fatal lactic acidosis are caused by mutations in COX subunits or assembly factors.

nodes.description: Biallelic SCO2 variants impair copper delivery to the CuA center of COX2, preventing assembly of a catalytically competent Complex IV holoenzyme.
nodes.id: SCO2 Loss and Defective Copper Delivery to COX

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: we have identified mutations in the human homologue, SCO2, in three unrelated infants with a newly recognized fatal cardioencephalomyopathy and COX deficiency.
